name: Browser Auto-Close
description: Auto-stops the OpenClaw-managed browser after an idle timeout (no cron).

stopTimeoutMs.description: Timeout (ms) to allow for each `openclaw browser stop` call.

stopMethod.description: How to stop the browser when idle. cli = use `openclaw browser stop` (recommended). http = POST /stop on browser controlUrl.

profile.description: Deprecated (fallback only). In cli mode we stop all running profiles dynamically.

maxTabs.description: Hard cap: if a profile exceeds this many tabs, browser-autoclose will force-stop + reset that profile (prevents runaway memory).

maxParentsPerProfile.description: Safety cap: maximum number of top-level Chrome parents allowed per profile user-data-dir. If exceeded, the plugin will force-stop that profile.

guardIntervalMs.description: How often to run the safety guard checks (parents/rogue CDP; maxTabs checked separately).

enforceNoManualCdp18800.description: If true, kill any Chrome parent using --remote-debugging-port=18800 that is NOT using the OpenClaw openclaw profile user-data-dir. This prevents the manual Playwright-on-18800 workaround from wedging OpenClaw.

timeoutCooldownMs.description: If a browser tool call fails due to browser control service timeout, block further browser calls for this cooldown window (circuit breaker) and attempt soft recovery without restarting the gateway.

restartChromeProfileOnTimeout.description: On browser control timeout, stop the internal 'chrome' profile (relay/control) as part of soft recovery.

restartOpenclawProfileOnTimeout.description: On browser control timeout, stop the 'openclaw' profile as part of soft recovery.

browserToolTimeoutMs.description: Minimum timeout to apply to browser tool calls (via before_tool_call). Helps avoid spurious 20s control-service timeouts under load.

resetProfileOnForceStop.description: If true, whenever the guard force-stops a profile (e.g. maxTabs/maxParents exceeded), also run `openclaw browser reset-profile` so Chrome won't restore a huge tab/session set next start.

resetProfileOnTimeout.description: If true, after a browser control service timeout, soft-recovery will reset the openclaw profile so we don't restore runaway tabs after recovery.

profilesCheckIntervalMs.description: How often to run `openclaw browser profiles --json` to enforce maxTabs (kept infrequent).

memAvailableThresholdMb.description: If MemAvailable drops below this threshold, run an immediate profiles check and enforce maxTabs.

maxParentsPerProfile.label: Max parent Chrome processes per profile
